description: Chain-GDP registry. 25 chains tracked. Each entry maps our slug to: DL fees endpoint name (dl_chain), DL summary/fees slug for REV (dl_rev_slug), DL stablecoin chainCirculating key (dl_stable_key), and CoinGecko native-token id (cg_id). Chains without a native token (Base/edgeX/Ink/zkLighter/Plasma/MegaETH/Katana) carry cg_id=null and drop out of GDP Multiple. CG ids for newer chains (Monad, Plasma) may need verification on first run.
methodology_notes: GDP = sum dailyRevenue across protocols on chain, excluding category=Chain (those are infrastructure / REV, not app-layer)
Stablecoin attribution INCLUDED in primary GDP per paper base case: (USDC chain share × Circle daily revenue) + (USDT chain share × Tether daily revenue)
ETF attribution EXCLUDED from primary GDP per paper §3.2.2 (the Python tracker silently includes it; we follow the paper instead)
Off-chain 'other' attribution EXCLUDED — heuristic with weak justification (paper doesn't endorse it as base case)
REV = dailyFees from /summary/fees/{slug} — captures base + priority fees
GDP Multiple = mcap / (gdp_30d × 365/30). Bands: <10× exceptional, 10–50× fair, 50–200× expensive, >200× speculative

hyperliquid.name: Hyperliquid
hyperliquid.structural_note: App-chain where Hyperliquid Perps IS effectively the chain's only major app — REV (validator/sequencer fees) and GDP (app revenue) overlap structurally, so REV/GDP isn't comparable to general-purpose chains.

polygon.name: Polygon
polygon.structural_note: MATIC → POL migration ongoing — market cap reading reflects POL only; interpret GDP Multiple with care.

edgex.name: edgeX
edgex.structural_note: App-chain (single-protocol). REV/GDP not applicable; no native token, so no GDP Multiple.

zklighter.name: zkLighter
zklighter.structural_note: App-chain. Hosts the Lighter Perps protocol — REV/GDP overlap similar to Hyperliquid; LIT is the same token tracked under /protocols/lighter.
